transcript.final_response: Great! Now I understand - I need to use parameter expansion instead of regex. Let me rewrite the script:
tool_calls:
cat /data/commits.log

# Create the release directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /release

# Create arrays to store commits by type
declare -A features=()
declare -A bugfixes=()
declare -A other=()

# Read the commits log and parse each line
while IFS= read -r line; do
  # Extract type using regex: type(scope): message
  if [[ $line =~ ^([a-z]+)\(([^)]+)\):[[:space:]]*(.*)$ ]]; then
    type="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
    scope="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    message="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    
    # Group by type
    case "$type" in
      feat)
        features["$scope"]="$message"
        ;;
      fix)
        bugfixes["$scope"]="$message"
        ;;
      *)
        other["$scope:$type"]="$message"
        ;;
    esac
  fi
done < /data/commits.log

# Generate the CHANGELOG.md file
{
  echo "# Changelog"
  echo ""
  
  # Features section
  if [ ${#features[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "## Features"
    echo ""
    for scope in $(echo "${!features[@]}" | tr ' ' '\n' | sort); do
      echo "- $scope: ${features[$scope]}"
    done
    echo ""
  fi
  
  # Bug Fixes section
  if [ ${#bugfixes[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "## Bug Fixes"
    echo ""
    for scope in $(echo "${!bugfixes[@]}" | tr ' ' '\n' | sort); do
      echo "- $scope: ${bugfixes[$scope]}"
    done
    echo ""
  fi
  
  # Other section
  if [ ${#other[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "## Other"
    echo ""
    for scope in $(echo "${!other[@]}" | tr ' ' '\n' | sort); do
      echo "- $scope: ${other[$scope]}"
    done
    echo ""
  fi
} > /release/CHANGELOG.md

# Display the generated changelog
echo "Generated CHANGELOG.md:"
echo "======================="
cat /release/CHANGELOG.md

# Create temporary files to store commits by type
mkdir -p /release
> /tmp/features.txt
> /tmp/bugfixes.txt
> /tmp/other.txt

# Read the commits log and parse each line
while IFS= read -r line; do
  # Extract type using regex: type(scope): message
  if [[ $line =~ ^([a-z]+)\(([^)]+)\):[[:space:]]*(.*)$ ]]; then
    type="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
    scope="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    message="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    
    # Group by type
    case "$type" in
      feat)
        echo "- $scope: $message" >> /tmp/features.txt
        ;;
      fix)
        echo "- $scope: $message" >> /tmp/bugfixes.txt
        ;;
      *)
        echo "- $scope ($type): $message" >> /tmp/other.txt
        ;;
    esac
  fi
done < /data/commits.log

# Generate the CHANGELOG.md file
{
  echo "# Changelog"
  echo ""
  
  # Features section
  if [ -s /tmp/features.txt ]; then
    echo "## Features"
    echo ""
    sort /tmp/features.txt
    echo ""
  fi
  
  # Bug Fixes section
  if [ -s /tmp/bugfixes.txt ]; then
    echo "## Bug Fixes"
    echo ""
    sort /tmp/bugfixes.txt
    echo ""
  fi
  
  # Other section
  if [ -s /tmp/other.txt ]; then
    echo "## Other"
    echo ""
    sort /tmp/other.txt
    echo ""
  fi
} > /release/CHANGELOG.md

# Display the generated changelog
echo "Generated CHANGELOG.md:"
echo "======================="
cat /release/CHANGELOG.md

# Test the regex parsing first
while IFS= read -r line; do
  echo "Processing: $line"
  if [[ $line =~ ^([a-z]+)\(([^)]+)\):[[:space:]]*(.*)$ ]]; then
    type="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
    scope="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    message="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    echo "  Type: $type | Scope: $scope | Message: $message"
  else
    echo "  NO MATCH"
  fi
done < /data/commits.log

# Check for special characters
hexdump -C /data/commits.log | head -20

# Create temporary files to store commits by type
mkdir -p /release
> /tmp/features.txt
> /tmp/bugfixes.txt
> /tmp/other.txt

# Read the commits log and parse each line
while IFS= read -r line; do
  # Extract type using a simpler regex
  if [[ $line =~ ^([a-z]+)\(([^)]+)\):(.*)$ ]]; then
    type="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
    scope="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    message="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    # Remove leading whitespace from message
    message="${message#[[:space:]]}"
    
    echo "Parsed: type=$type, scope=$scope, message=$message" >&2
    
    # Group by type
    case "$type" in
      feat)
        echo "- $scope: $message" >> /tmp/features.txt
        ;;
      fix)
        echo "- $scope: $message" >> /tmp/bugfixes.txt
        ;;
      *)
        echo "- $scope ($type): $message" >> /tmp/other.txt
        ;;
    esac
  fi
done < /data/commits.log

echo "Files created:" >&2
ls -la /tmp/*.txt >&2

echo "Contents:" >&2
echo "=== Features ===" >&2
cat /tmp/features.txt >&2
echo "=== Bug Fixes ===" >&2
cat /tmp/bugfixes.txt >&2
echo "=== Other ===" >&2
cat /tmp/other.txt >&2

# Test simple parsing
head -1 /data/commits.log | od -c | head -20

line="feat(auth): add OAuth2 support"
# Test with extended glob
if [[ $line =~ ^[a-z]+\([^)]+\):.*$ ]]; then
  echo "MATCHED basic pattern"
fi

# Test with capture groups
if [[ $line =~ ^([a-z]+) ]]; then
  echo "Type matches: ${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
fi

# Try a different approach using parameter expansion
type_part="${line%%(*}"
echo "Type part: $type_part"

scope_part="${line#*(}"
scope_part="${scope_part%%)*}"
echo "Scope part: $scope_part"

message_part="${line#*): }"
echo "Message part: $message_part"

# Create temporary files to store commits by type
mkdir -p /release
> /tmp/features.txt
> /tmp/bugfixes.txt
> /tmp/other.txt

# Read the commits log and parse each line
while IFS= read -r line; do
  # Skip empty lines
  [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
  
  # Extract type: everything before the first (
  type="${line%%(*}"
  
  # Extract scope: everything between ( and )
  scope="${line#*(}"

  message="${line#*): }"
  
  # Group by type
  case "$type" in
    feat)
      echo "- $scope: $message" >> /tmp/features.txt
      ;;
    fix)
      echo "- $scope: $message" >> /tmp/bugfixes.txt
      ;;
    *)
      echo "- $scope ($type): $message" >> /tmp/other.txt
      ;;
  esac
done < /data/commits.log

# Generate the CHANGELOG.md file
{
  echo "# Changelog"
  echo ""
  
  # Features section
  if [ -s /tmp/features.txt ]; then
    echo "## Features"
    echo ""
    sort /tmp/features.txt
    echo ""
  fi
  
  # Bug Fixes section
  if [ -s /tmp/bugfixes.txt ]; then
    echo "## Bug Fixes"
    echo ""
    sort /tmp/bugfixes.txt
    echo ""
  fi
  
  # Other section
  if [ -s /tmp/other.txt ]; then
    echo "## Other"
    echo ""
    sort /tmp/other.txt
    echo ""
  fi
} > /release/CHANGELOG.md

# Display the generated changelog
echo "Generated CHANGELOG.md:"
echo "======================="
cat /release/CHANGELOG.md

feat(auth): add OAuth2 support
fix(api): handle null response body
feat(ui): dark mode toggle

fix(ui): button alignment on mobile
docs(readme): update install instructions
feat(db): add connection pooling
fix(api): correct status code for 404

=======================
# Changelog

## Features

- api: add pagination support
- auth: add OAuth2 support
- db: add connection pooling
- ui: dark mode toggle

## Bug Fixes

- api: correct status code for 404
- api: handle null response body
- auth: token refresh race condition
- ui: button alignment on mobile

## Other

- deps (chore): update lodash to 4.17.21
- readme (docs): update install instructions
